data.title: Ethereum L1 Positioned as Global Ledger, Says Vitalik Buterin
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin shared a tweet emphasizing the role of Ethereum Layer 1 (L1) as the world's ledger. The tweet highlighted Ethereum L1's potential to serve as the primary ledger globally, allowing anyone to access, use, and add data or functionalities without permission.

The tweet described Ethereum L1 as possessing credible neutrality, censorship resistance, and tamper-proof qualities, with visibility of any alterations achieved through penalty mechanisms and transparency. It emphasized the ongoing gradual decentralization of Ethereum.

Furthermore, the tweet praised Ethereum's vast global community, which remains vigilant against attempts to undermine the system. As Ethereum continues to grow and mature, such attempts are expected to become increasingly challenging.

data.title: Jupiter DEX Pauses Governance Voting to Focus on Growth
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the Solana-based Jupiter decentralized exchange (DEX) has announced a temporary halt to its governance voting process. Kash Dhanda, the chief operating officer of Jupiter, revealed this decision in a detailed statement on Thursday, emphasizing the need for the protocol to concentrate on growth. Dhanda described the current moment as a critical juncture for decentralized finance (DeFi), noting that the opportunity to shape its future is fleeting.

Dhanda explained that the decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) structure is not functioning as intended, leading to a decision to suspend DAO votes until 2026. The aim is to return with a more cohesive approach that fosters unity rather than division. He pointed out that the DAO is caught in a negative feedback loop, which hampers execution and creates discord within the community. By pausing DAO voting, Jupiter intends to prioritize execution, speed, and growth while re-evaluating the optimal operation of the DAO.

The suspension will not impact active staking rewards, and all previously funded work groups will continue their operations. However, no new proposals will be accepted, and the community reserve will remain untouched until voting resumes. The development team plans to support community growth using its operational treasury. DAO voting is expected to resume next year after Jupiter's team establishes a new process through community engagement, aiming for a more effective governance model. Dhanda reiterated that this is a temporary pause, not an end to governance.

This decision comes amid a broader trend of reevaluating DAO governance structures. Earlier this month, Yuga Labs proposed an overhaul of the ApeCoin ecosystem, suggesting the closure of the ApeCoin DAO in favor of a new entity called ApeCo. Greg Solano, CEO of Yuga Labs, argued that the DAO had become dysfunctional, slowing development and devolving into ineffective governance. He criticized the allocation of resources to low-impact initiatives and vanity proposals, echoing concerns similar to those expressed by Dhanda. The ongoing discussions highlight the challenges and potential limitations of DAO governance in the rapidly evolving DeFi landscape.

data.title: Phoenix Group Plans Expansion and Dual Listing Amid AI Growth
data.description:
According to PANews, Phoenix Group, a Bitcoin mining company based in Abu Dhabi, is planning to double its global data center capacity to over 1 gigawatt within the next two years. This expansion aims to venture into the artificial intelligence sector, with considerations to relocate part of its operations to the United States. Munaf Ali, CEO of Phoenix Group, highlighted the strategic advantage of combining Bitcoin mining, which can be operational within six months, with AI data centers that require years to build, as a means to mitigate risks.

Phoenix Group intends to separate its mining and data center operations and pursue a dual listing on a U.S. exchange. The parent company in Abu Dhabi will retain majority ownership. Although Ali did not specify a timeline, he emphasized that dual listing would facilitate funding and capitalize on the higher valuations of U.S. tech companies.

In December of last year, Phoenix Group announced plans for a dual listing in the U.S. by 2025.

data.title: Cantina Introduces Web3SOC Framework for DeFi Project Evaluation
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Cantina has announced the launch of Web3SOC, a standardized assessment framework designed for institutional and decentralized finance (DeFi) projects. The initiative aims to foster a secure and compliant decentralized financial ecosystem. The framework was developed in collaboration with several projects, including Uniswap Labs, Morpho, Maple Finance, Kiln, Steakhouse Financial, L1D, and Secureum.

Web3SOC evaluates projects across four dimensions: operations, finance, security, and compliance. It categorizes projects into four maturity levels: Tier 1 (enterprise-level), Tier 2 (mature), Tier 3 (growth), and Tier 4 (early-stage). This system can be utilized by institutions for due diligence and offers project teams a path for self-assessment and optimization. Projects can obtain ratings through Cantina, and the framework will continue to evolve to meet the needs of the DeFi industry.

data.title: Bitcoin Could Crash to $92K as Demand Drops 50%, CryptoQuant Warns – Is the Bull Run Over?
data.description:
Bitcoin faces a potential crash to $92,000 as demand metrics show alarming deterioration across multiple key indicators, with CryptoQuant's head of research Julio Moreno warning that Bitcoin has entered a "soft patch" that could derail the current bull run as apparent demand growth has plummeted by nearly 50% from its May peak of 228,000 BTC to just 118,000 BTC over the past 30 days.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoin-could-crash-to-92k-as-demand-drops-50-cryptoquant-warns-is-the-bull-run-over/

data.title: Fed Governor Waller sends a clear dovish signal: in favor of a rate cut in July
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed Governor Waller recently said that he agrees that a rate cut should be considered in July, and believes that tariffs will not lead to sustained inflation. He said that tariffs will be a one-time factor and the Fed should not wait until the job market collapses before cutting interest rates. Waller said that at present, the job market is stable, but some signs are beginning to appear, such as the high unemployment rate of fresh graduates. For six months, the Fed has been waiting and waiting for the inflation shock to come. Waller believes that the Fed has room to lower interest rates and then see what happens with inflation. Waller said that the Fed may be in a position to cut interest rates as early as July. Before Waller made these remarks, the market bet that the Fed would only have a 14% chance of cutting interest rates in July. (Jinshi)

data.title: 50% of Marathon's mining machine orders this year will come from the US company Auradin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Marathon CEO said that about half of this year's mining machine orders came from Auradin, a US Bitcoin chip designer and manufacturer. MARA is one of Auradin's earliest supporters. Auradin began shipping the Teraflux series of Bitcoin ASICs in mid-2024, positioning it as a US-made alternative to Chinese ASIC giants Bitmain and Shenma. Since US President Trump announced new tariffs in April, Auradin has received "a large number of requests from US mining companies who want to diversify their hardware equipment."

data.title: Access Protocol Warns of Potential Scam Following Account Hack
data.description: According to PANews, Access Protocol has issued a warning on the X platform regarding a recent hack of its creator Elogiso's account. The organization cautions users about potential scammers who may reach out with fraudulent collaboration requests, such as podcast interviews, containing malicious links. Users are advised to remain vigilant and report any suspicious messages by sending a direct message to the official account for verification.

data.title: Sei Network Chosen as Candidate Blockchain for WYST Stablecoin
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Sei Network has been selected by the Wyoming Stablecoin Commission as a candidate blockchain for WYST, a U.S. stablecoin backed by fiat currency. The stablecoin will utilize LayerZero's cross-chain bridging technology.

data.title: JuChain Ecosystem's first metaverse city, Fomo City, attracted more than 10,000 on-chain users within 5 hours of its launch
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Fomo City, the first native metaverse co-governance city of JuChain ecosystem, has officially started the first city construction phase at 13:14:19 today. It has attracted more than 10,000+ on-chain users to participate in the first phase of the city co-construction in just 5 hours.
Fomo City is a key metaverse ecological project jointly promoted by JuChain and JuCoin, dedicated to creating a new on-chain digital civilization sample co-built, co-governed and shared by the community. Users can obtain identity credentials, construction permissions, long-term benefits and governance rights by holding FMC tokens.

data.title: Tesla Secures $557M Energy Storage Deal with Shanghai as Energy Division Grows
data.description:
Neither the author, Tim Fries, nor this website, The Tokenist, provide financial advice. Please consult our website policy prior to making financial decisions. Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) has reportedly inked a massive $557 million energy storage station deal with Shanghai authorities, marking the electric vehicle giant’s first grid-scale battery storage project in China. The agreement, signed Friday between Tesla, China Kangfu International Leasing Co, and the Shanghai government, involves a 4 billion yuan investment and will utilize Tesla’s Megapack batteries for large-scale energy infrastructure
source: https://tokenist.com/tesla-secures-557m-energy-storage-deal-with-shanghai-as-energy-division-grows/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-secures-557m-energy-storage-deal-with-shanghai-as-energy-division-grows
